chapter_title: Articles of leather; saddlery and harness; travel goods, handbags and similar containers; articles of animal gut (other than silk-worm gut)
inclusions: Heading 42.01 — Saddlery and harness for ANY animal (including traces, leads, knee pads, muzzles, saddle cloths, saddle bags, dog coats and the like), of ANY material

Heading 42.02 — Trunks, suit-cases, vanity-cases, executive-cases, brief-cases, school satchels, spectacle cases, binocular cases, camera cases, musical instrument cases, gun cases, holsters and similar containers; travelling-bags, insulated food/beverage bags, toilet bags, rucksacks, handbags, shopping-bags, wallets, purses, map-cases, cigarette-cases, tobacco-pouches, tool bags, sports bags, bottle-cases, jewellery boxes, powder-boxes, cutlery cases and similar containers — of leather/composition leather, plastics sheeting, textile materials, vulcanised fibre or paperboard, or wholly/mainly covered with such materials or paper. Subheadings classified by: outer surface material (leather; plastics/textile; other) AND container type.

Heading 42.03 — Articles of apparel and clothing accessories of leather or composition leather: Note 4 defines scope — includes gloves (sports, protective), mittens, mitts, aprons, protective clothing, braces, belts, bandoliers, wrist straps. Excludes watch straps (91.13)

Heading 42.05 — Other articles of leather or of composition leather: boot laces, mats, industrial safety belts/harnesses, leather strings/cords for jewellery, articles for machinery/technical uses, other
Heading 42.06 — Articles of gut (other than silk-worm gut), of goldbeater's skin, of bladders or of tendons

exclusions.condition: Sterile surgical catgut or similar sterile suture materials
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 30.06 (pharmaceutical products for surgery) — Note 2(a)

exclusions.condition: Articles of apparel or clothing accessories (EXCEPT gloves, mittens, mitts) lined with furskin or artificial fur, OR to which furskin/artificial fur is attached on the OUTSIDE (beyond mere trimming)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 43.03 (genuine furskin clothing) or 43.04 (artificial fur clothing) — Note 2(b). Exception: gloves/mittens/mitts lined with fur STAY in 42.03
Redirect to Chapter 64 — Note 2(d). Leather shoes, boots, sandals even if made of same materials as 42.02 articles → Ch.64

exclusions.condition: Headgear or parts thereof (including caps, hats, crash helmets with leather cover)
Cuff-links, bracelets or other imitation jewellery made of leather
Metal fittings or trimmings for harness (stirrups, bits, horse brasses, buckles) presented separately
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV (base metals) generally — Note 2(h). Only when assembled as part of a harness do they go to 42.01

exclusions.condition: Strings, skins for drums or other parts of musical instruments
Toys, games, sports requisites (except leather sports gloves which stay in 42.03)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 95 — Note 2(l). Note: leather sports gloves (baseball, boxing) stay in 42.03

exclusions.condition: Buttons, press-fasteners, snap-fasteners, press-studs, button moulds or blanks
Bags made of sheeting of plastics with handles, NOT designed for prolonged use — i.e., disposable plastic shopping bags
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 39.23 (plastic packing articles) — Note 3(A)(a). 42.02 requires DURABLE bags designed for prolonged use

exclusions.condition: Articles of plaiting materials (baskets, wicker bags, straw bags)
Watch straps — even if of leather
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 91.13 — Note 4. Leather watch straps explicitly excluded from 42.03

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Definition of 'leather' for Chapter 42 (broader than everyday use)
classification_rules.description: In Chapter 42, 'leather' includes: (a) regular tanned leather; (b) chamois (including combination chamois) leather; (c) patent leather; (d) patent laminated leather; (e) metallised leather. All special-finish leathers of heading 41.14 are treated as 'leather' for purposes of classifying in Ch.42.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3(B) — Precious metal parts in 42.02/42.03 do NOT automatically move to Ch.71
classification_rules.description: Articles of headings 42.02 and 42.03 that have PARTS of precious metal, cultured pearls, precious/semi-precious stones: STAY in 42.02/42.03 even if these parts are beyond minor fittings or ornamentation — PROVIDED they do not give the article its essential character. If the precious parts give the article its essential character (e.g., a solid gold-framed handbag) → classify in Chapter 71. The 'essential character' test is the deciding trigger.

classification_rules.rule: Heading 42.02 — outer surface material determines subheading
classification_rules.description: Within heading 42.02, classification at the subheading level is driven by the OUTER SURFACE MATERIAL: (1) Leather or composition leather (subheadings x1); (2) Sheeting of plastics or textile materials (subheadings x2); (3) Other — vulcanised fibre, paperboard, other (subheadings x9). The outer surface is assessed from the exterior of the container as presented, not the lining or frame.

classification_rules.rule: Heading 42.02 — 'prolonged use' requirement; NOT for disposable bags
classification_rules.description: Heading 42.02 covers only containers designed for PROLONGED USE (durable goods). Per Note 3(A)(a), bags of plastic sheeting with handles NOT designed for prolonged use (single-use/thin disposable carrier bags) → 39.23. The durability and design intent is the test. Thick reusable shopping bags of plastic → 42.02; thin disposable carrier bags → 39.23.

Note 4 explicitly defines scope of 42.03 'articles of apparel and clothing accessories': gloves (sports AND protective work gloves), mittens, mitts; aprons and other protective clothing; braces, belts, bandoliers; wrist straps. DOES NOT include watch straps (→ 91.13). Sports leather gloves stay in 42.03, not Chapter 95.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 42.01 (saddlery/harness assembled) vs Section XV (metal harness fittings separately presented)
classification_rules.description: Heading 42.01 captures complete saddlery/harness/animal equipment of ANY material as assembled or ready articles. When metal fittings (stirrups, bits, buckles) are presented SEPARATELY (not attached to a harness) → they go to Section XV base metals or Section XIV precious metals. The presentation state (assembled vs. components) determines classification.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 42.02 (durable travel containers) vs 39.23 (plastic packaging) vs 46.02 (plaiting material articles)
classification_rules.description: Three competing headings for bag-type articles: 42.02 = durable bags/cases of leather, plastic, textile, vulcanised fibre or paperboard (designed for prolonged use). 39.23 = bags of plastic sheeting as packaging/disposable. 46.02 = bags of plaiting materials (wicker, rattan, bamboo, straw — basketwork). Identify material + durability to route correctly.
